unexpected_finds.title: Q-196-4 checklist is auto-implementable as string-scan pass; could become a CLI/operator pre-check script
unexpected_finds.detail: All 8 checklist items reduce to either (a) case-insensitive substring search against a curated blocklist, (b) structural check on enumeration form (file:line vs vague total), or (c) language pattern check (build invariant vs subjective milestone). This is implementable as ~50 lines of shell / Python. If implemented as scripts/check_m10_commitment.sh in wallis-pcf-lean4, the Phase A.1.5 fire becomes a 2-second CI-style pass rather than a synth consultation, freeing the synth dispatch budget for genuinely judgment-tier work.
unexpected_finds.recommended_action: Optional operator-tier task; bundled with UF-195-1 pre-commit hook automation (both fall under wallis-pcf-lean4 .git/hooks or scripts/).

unexpected_finds.title: Reject-and-consult default as SIARC discipline rule for novel category encounters
unexpected_finds.detail: Q-196 summary states: 'if the operator introduces a category of statement not anticipated in the EXCLUDE list, the synth reviewer should default to reject and consult rather than approve. The EXCLUDE list is exhaustive over currently-known RULE-1 axes but cannot pre-empt novel admin-tier framings.' This generalizes: whenever a mechanical-checklist pass encounters a novel category not enumerated in the source verdict, default-action = reject + escalate to synth consultation, NOT default-action = approve. This is a SIARC discipline rule worth codifying.
unexpected_finds.memory_text_proposed: For SIARC mechanical-checklist Phase-A1.5-style gates: when the paragraph/artefact under review contains a category not enumerated in the source-verdict EXCLUDE/INCLUDE lists, the default action is REJECT + escalate to synth consultation, NOT APPROVE. Exhaustive lists cannot pre-empt novel framings; reject-and-consult is the safe default.

unexpected_finds.title: 7-point INCLUDE template is paste-ready for operator paragraph-fill; reduces Phase A.1 substrate-prep wallclock
unexpected_finds.detail: Q-196-2 delivers a bracketed-slot skeleton that operator can fill in 30-60 minutes of focused work (vs hours of un-scaffolded drafting). This advances m10-commitment-paragraph todo from PENDING DRAFT to PENDING FILL_BRACKETS, materially reducing the Phase A.1 wallclock once Lean axis unblocks. Operator may proceed with paragraph-fill in parallel to OPT_X2 cache-repair work per PROMPT 194 Q-194-3 (c)-parallel verdict.

unexpected_finds.title: Verdict labels the 4 borderline cases self-consistently with template structure
unexpected_finds.detail: BC-1 (AEAL count) → point 2 of template; BC-2 (L284 simp) → point 4; BC-3 (M11/M12 cite) → point 5 with condition; BC-4 (clean-clone reproducibility) → point 7. Synth coupled the borderline dispositions to specific template slots. This makes the INCLUDE/EXCLUDE/CONDITIONAL_INCLUDE verdicts immediately operationalizable rather than abstract policy.
